observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
1.12 We recommended and Management agreed that the MGSO and the Inventory and Disposal Committee:
Expedite the process of transferring the properties that are located in and used by other government agencies to its users;
Pursue its plan to implement the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
Facilitate the immediate disposal of unserviceable and obsolete properties and furnish the CAO with the necessary supporting documents for its derecognition from the books.

Recommendations:
4.9    We recommended and Management agreed that:
the MEO submit the CoC and other necessary supporting documents to the                  MACCO as soon as the project is completed, to ensure that the recording of infrastructure projects are properly supported with documentary requirements; and
the Municipal Accountant record the depreciation expenses of the 12 completed projects (see Annex H) in the ensuing year:

Recommendations:
6.6    We recommended and Management agreed to:
include the timelines specifying the implementation period of the project from the release of funds to the submission of liquidation documents; and
impose the liquidation of fund transfers in compliance with the provisions in the MOA and if necessary, impose penalty/sanctions for non-compliance therewith, taking into consideration Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code which states that “a public officer who fails to account for public funds or property upon demand by an authorized officer shall be presumed to have used the fund or property for personal gain.”

Recommendations:
7.8    We recommended and Management agreed that the MDRRM Officer:
promptly execute the indicated PPAs within the time frame prescribed in the LDRRMFIP; and
closely monitor and review the implementation of the programmed PPAs in the LDRRMFIP, and take necessary actions such as revising the implementation plan, reallocating resources, or identifying alternative strategies to ensure that disaster risk reduction and management objectives are effectively met.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to consider the availment of the one-time cleansing of PPEs to facilitate the immediate processing of reports and relief of accountability as to unidentified and damaged PPEs.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Compel the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of all PPEs in CY 2024 and identify those assets that are no longer controlled by the Municipality;
Request for approval from the local Sanggunian for the transfer of the subject properties to the recipient agencies and direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting journal entries; and
Implement the one-time cleansing of PPE.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management direct the MACCO and the MGSO to perform the following:
Conduct reconciliation of records and reports of both offices in order to ensure an accurate balance of the Road Networks account;
Ensure agreement of Accounting Records and General Services Records with respect to the result of conducted physical count of inventories and PPEs; and
Conduct an annual physical inventory count of road networks in a timely manner to allow sufficient time for reconciliation with the accounting records. Subsequently, submit the latter to the Office of the Auditor for validation.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer the unutilized 30% QRF and DRRM-MOOE for CY 2023 from the general fund to trust fund books with the following entries:

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the personnel concerned to reapply for fidelity bonds as SDOs; and
Grant cash advances for CF only to bonded SDOs.

Recommendations:
We recommended Management to:
Require the MEO to closely monitor the implementation of the project and ensure that all remaining works as programmed in the POW are fulfilled;
Refrain from issuing Certificate of Completion to infrastructure projects in which construction is still ongoing; and
Diligently conduct procurement planning and prioritize those projects that are funded with automatic reversions.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MEO to:
Adhere to Items 10 and 11 in the IRR of RA 9184 on work suspension and granting time extension to contractors; and
Conduct proper validation and assessment of projects to ensure that these are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready, thus difficulties faced by the contractor such as material transportation to the project site should be considered before the implementation.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004, and instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to –
Deduct from the retention pay the cost of billboards for projects in Barangays Asinan and Saub; and
Require the attachment of photos of the installed project billboards at project sites upon payment of mobilization cost or first progress billing. Information contained in the billboard should be visible in the photos so that it can be determined that it is installed on the project site; and
Municipal Engineering Office and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to require contractors to install project billboards at the project location containing complete information, design and format in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 and its Annexes.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to complete the process on the imposition of sanctions against JOCRIS Industries in accordance with the Uniform Guidelines for Blacklisting of Manufacturers, Suppliers, Distributors, Contractors and Consultants.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management:
Conduct a thorough review of the necessity and validity of the programmed PPAs in the LDRRMP before formulating the AIP as the basis for the Annual Budget;
Ensure to incorporate the programmed PPAs in the LDRRMP to the AIP in strict adherence to the above-mentioned guidelines; and
Ensure that PPAs are properly presented as to components (70% Pre-disaster, 30% QRF), and the nature of expenditures (MOOE and CO).

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management consider the sourcing of funds from the Barangay Development Program under the 20% Development Fund for the procurement of water pipes and other plumbing supplies to make the PWS operational.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the MEO and the BAC to evaluate the feasibility of the completion of the project; and
Assess the manpower complement of the MEO vis-à-vis its workload, and determine if hiring of additional engineer/s is warranted.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO and other concerned offices to fully utilize the cash donations totaling P2,715,105.20 for their intended purpose in the ensuing year. Otherwise, remit it to the BTr if there are no plans to use such donated funds.

Conduct verification, analysis, validation and reconciliation of the above mentioned dormant receivable accounts. If needed, effect adjustments as allowed in Item 7.2 and 7.3, COA Circular No. 2016-005 and does not need to be submitted to COA for approval but subject to the usual audit. Send demand letters to debtors/accountable persons in coordination with the MSWDO for the Grow Micro Lending Program and to consider write-off of dormant receivables only as a last resort by reason of public/state policy. File appropriate request for authority to write-off dormant accounts with the Commission on Audit once collectability remained uncertain. Henceforward, regularly and periodically conduct verification, analysis, validation, reconciliation and render report of the existing receivable accounts.
observations.reason: The management had not submitted any copy of the demand letter sent to individuals/beneficiaries. Also, no request for write-off has been transmitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Planning and Development Office or the Municipal Engineering Office for the immediate preparation of the documentary requirements and the submission thereof to the concerned office as required in the 6.5.4 of the DILG Memorandum Circular
observations.reason: Notarized LGU Certification of Compliance to project Implementation was not yet submitted

observations.recommendation: Consider redirecting priorities from regular programs and activities to other major concerns that needs immediate action by the LGU especially purchasing of lots for relocation sites of those homeless victims of calamity; Require the LDRRM Officer to fully implement on CY 2020 the unimplemented projects, programs and activities of the Annual Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan and Budget for CY 2019.
observations.reason: Only a small amount of the 2019 cash donations remained.

observations.recommendation: Maintain disposal site records containing the monitoring of water and air quality; Submit a written notification containing the names, addresses and contact numbers of sanitary landfill site personnel to the appropriate agencies; Designate only one area for waste sorting and maintain cleanliness at all times within the perimeter of the RCA facility; and Procure appropriate protective equipment for those directly performing solid waste management operations.
observations.reason: MENRO permit is on-going processing. Will acquire certificati on from EMB to consider the 3 ponds built to filter water from the residual solid waste enough in monitoring the water quality.

observations.recommendation: Direct the OLEEM Manager to ensure that lease applications should only be approved after all the requirements are submitted and to cause the renewal of the lease contracts of all existing occupants, have them duly notarized, and ensure that requirements for such renewal are complied with.
observations.reason: There are still stallholder s with no lease contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Implementing Offices and other officials concerned to expedite the implementation and accomplishment of the remaining unimplemented and on-going projects funded under the 20% Development Fund so that such projects could immediately serve the purpose for which they were proposed. Should management sense that projects are not feasible for implementation, it may be practical to realign them to other viable projects. Moreover, proper planning and identification of doable development PPAs should always be considered by Management to ensure efficient and effective use of government resources.
observations.reason: Continuing PPAs are still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Development Council to rectify the errors of recording of the particular project into the 20% development fund in the AIP. We also recommended that projects/programs that will be included in the local budget shall be presented in details such as nature and particulars of the programs/projects and the recipients, among others, in the AIP, be it the original or the supplemental AIP to provide full, complete and precise information regarding those projects/programs.
observations.reason: The MDC has not yet rectify the errors of recording of the particular project into the 20% development fund in the AIP. Since no rectification of errors, there was no 2018 supplemental AIP on the particular project.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Local Economic Enterprise to strictly implement the payment of surcharge for the late payments and to disseminate to all lessees of the market on the imposition of surcharges for late payments in accordance with the Municipal Ordinance 171 s. 2012; addition of one market personnel.
observations.managementAction: Conscientious effort have been made and instituted by LEEM to imposed and have collected surcharges on late/delayed rental payments.
observations.reason: Collection of surcharges is still on-going. No collection for CY 2020 because of pandemic and earthquake.

observations.recommendation: Direct the MPDO, as well as the other departments involved in the implementation of development fund, to establish a system or process that facilitates monitoring of implementation of barangay projects funded from the 20% development fund of the municipality, to ensure optimal utilization of funds vis-à-vis accomplishments. The system should clearly define the role and function of each office to pinpoint responsibility.
observations.reason: The MPDO has still to implement a system of monitoring for the implementation of barangay projects and to include in the MOA, the barangay’s responsibility to submit a monthly monitoring report in the implementation of the support to barangay programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the project status of the PWS be determined, improvement or upgrading to be undertaken if necessary in order that the immediate utilization be made and its intended purpose be delivered and benefits be felt by the beneficiaries.
observations.reason: The leakage in the WPS was already repaired and was already utilized by Barangay New Visayas. However, with the DPWH road expansion, the water pipes were damaged.

observations.recommendation: To address the deficiency, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Treasurer facilitate a reevaluation and updating of the collection scheme employed by the Municipality and to extend extra effort to increase collection rate of both the current and delinquent receivables.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still on the process in establishing collection scheme to increase collection rate of the Municipality. There was slight increase in the RPT collection.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

We recommended to management the following:

   1. To create an Inventory Committee that will spearhead the conduct of  a
      complete and periodic physical count of all recorded local roads under
      the Municipality’s jurisdiction and shall prepare the  Report  on  the
      Physical Count of the Road Network System;



   2. To establish existence, and ensure timely submission of  the  required
      Report of Physical Count of Road Network  System,  so  that  necessary
      action could be effected if these local road networks are either  non-
      existing or shall form part of PPE. The report shall be  submitted  to
      the MACCO for subsequent reconciliation with the  local  road  network
      property cards;



   3. For the MGSO, MEO and the MACCO to evaluate the existing processes and
      identify those  roads  that  should  be  part  of  PPE;  to  reconcile
      inventory  records   of   both;   and   perform   their   duties   and
      responsibilities in accordance with  COA  Circular  2015-008  so  that
      records for local roads will be accounted for; and



   4. For MACCO to make  necessary  adjustments  to  correct  the  initially
      recorded balances of Road Networks and  Accumulated  Depreciation  and
      provide this office with copies of the adjustments made.

Recommendations:

We recommended Management to:


   1. Craft an internal accounting policy on the provision of impairment  of
      receivables that are long overdue and dormant and whose collectability
      is doubtful in accordance with IPSAS 29;



   2. For the Municipal Accountant to cause the  recognition  of  impairment
      for receivable accounts, and if warranted, for those receivables  with
      age from ten (10) years and above of which collection could no  longer
      be ascertained.



     A Request for a write-off for the derecognition of  the  accounts  may
     also  be  considered  following  the  procedures  provided  under  COA
     Circular No. 2016-005; and

  3. Henceforth, regular monitoring and analysis of receivables accounts be
     conducted to ensure that these are collected when these become due and
     demandable and that cash advances and fund  transfers  are  liquidated
     within the prescribed period depending upon their nature and purpose.

Recommendations:

We recommended Management to:

  a.  Request  the  accounting  software   provider   to   facilitate   the
     modification of the inventory valuation to the moving average  method;
     and



  b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the usage of  the  moving
     average method in the  valuation  of  inventories  and  to  facilitate
     identifying interventions, like manual computation of proper inventory
     valuation,  to  accurately  present  the  inventory  balance  in   the
     operational and financial reports  pending  the  modification  of  the
     accounting software.

Recommendations:

We recommended Management to:


  1. Fill up the following three (3) vacant  positions:  a.  Local  Revenue
     Collection Officer I, b. Revenue Collection Clerk II  and  c.  Revenue
     Collection Clerk II;




  2. If possible, recall all employees holding  treasury–related  positions
     detailed to other offices to discharge  the  collection  functions  as
     part of their duties and responsibilities inherent to their positions;
     and



  3. Ensure strict adherence to existing regulations that  all  accountable
     officers/employees including those employed as job order,  contractual
     and casual employees hired for the collection of revenues and issuance
     of receipts are properly bonded to safeguard government funds from the
     possible risk of loss/misuse.

Recommendations:

We recommended Management to:

     Direct the Municipal Accountant-


    1. Immediate submission of the above-lacking documents; and


    2.  Ensure  the  completeness  of  the  supporting   documents   in   the
       disbursement vouchers before submission to COA Audit Team.

Recommendations:

We recommended the following to Management:

   1. The Inspection Committee to carefully inspect goods received not  only
      as to the correctness of the quantity and specifications of the  goods
      received but also on the condition and the  remaining  shelf  life  of
      perishable goods;



   2. The MDRRMO, MSWDO and MGSO to coordinate closely and regularly for the
      proper monitoring  and  management  of  the  relief  and  other  goods
      acquired thru purchase or donation;



   3. Submit to the Audit Team  a  written  explanation  of  the  unreported
      damaged/spoiled goods;



   4. Distribute the remaining 189 food packs  without  the  rice  to  avoid
      spoilage; and



   5. Prepare a request for disposal of the spoiled rice for  its  immediate
      disposal.

Recommendations:

We recommended the Management to:

  1.  Install  a  sound  and  effective  internal   control   in   handling
     Inventories;




  2. Observe the guidelines set forth under COA Circular  No.  2014-002  on
     its future receipts of donation-in-kind;



  3. Ensure  clear-cut  guidelines  for  proper  coordination  between  the
     concerned offices, for proper maintenance  of  records,  stocking  and
     distribution of the inventories; and



  4. To consider in the future construction of a warehouse or stockroom for
     use by the MGSO.

Recommendations:

We recommended the Management to:

   1. Find alternatives like other near companies who catered these kinds of
      services with regards to the disposal of its stacked non-biodegradable
      wastes;




   2. Continue to comply with the mandatory provisions for the collection of
      non-recyclable materials and special wastes;



   3.  Conduct  regular  orientation  to  continually  educate   the   waste
      generators about the importance of proper waste segregation;



   4. Conduct necessary training to all collectors and personnel  to  ensure
      that solid wastes are handled properly  and  in  accordance  with  the
      guidelines pursuant to RA 9003; and



   5. Properly maintain the established Materials  Recovery  Facility  (MRF)
      within the Municipality in accordance with the prescribed guidelines.

adopt the most appropriate accounting policy on recognition of impairment losses considering the availability of data, the Post-Earthquake Rapid Structural Assessment Form already prepared by the MEO and nature of the impairment and to: Direct the Municipal Accountant to- Record the impairment loss for minor, moderate and severely damaged buildings and other structures; Direct the Municipal Engineer to- Complete the preparation of the report on the final list of damaged properties, if there is a need aside from the once prepared Post-Earthquake Rapid Structural Assessment Form, as the basis of the Sangguniang Bayan to drop some of the damaged properties.

Create a General Service Office or Property and Supply Office responsible for maintaining the records of the Municipality’s properties and supplies, if applicable within the budget. Direct the responsible office to comply the submission of the Report on Physical Count not later than January 31 of the following year; and Direct the Municipal Accountant to- Perform reconciliation of the subsidiary ledgers with the Report on Physical of Inventories and PPE (when already prepared by the Municipal Treasurer and General Service Officer).
observations.reason: There is no office created yet for General Service Office but the management already assigned a GSO designate

observations.recommendation: In coordination with the Offices of the Municipal Accountant, the General Services and the Municipal Health, reacquaint themselves of the established procedural mechanism or process flow from the receipt of the delivery of the transferred items from the Department of Health up to the recording of these inventories in the LGU’s books; Require the Municipal Health Officer to furnish the General Services Office with the Requisition and Issue Slip to facilitate the issuance and distribution of inventories; Direct the General Services Officer to prepare the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued, a monthly summary of RIS, and forwarded the same to the Accounting Office as basis in drawing a journal voucher to record the issuances; and Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the inventories on hand as of year-end balances.
observations.reason: The GSO designate will comply this CY 2022

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant- Immediate submission of the above-lacking documents; and Ensure the completeness of the supporting documents in the disbursement vouchers before submission to COA Audit Team. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Bids and Awards Committee- Acquaint themselves on the latest government issuances to ensure all documents are submitted by the winning suppliers and that are attached to the disbursement vouchers.

Direct the BAC Secretariat to comply with the posting requirements as required by the GPPB issuances in posting the notices of award, contracts or purchase orders so that transparency on the procurement process is attained. Ensure that printout copies on the postings made in the online portal of all notices of award, approved contracts or purchase orders be included among the supporting documents to be attached to the disbursement vouchers in all payment on procurements made.

that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguninan Bayan to identify the benefits that may be given to employees, subject to PS limitations and if warranted, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer and to provide the benefits due to them subject to the financial capability of the local government unit.

Conduct in-house seminar/briefing among the personnel involve in the procurement, utilization and documentation of fuel and motor vehicles vis-à-vis COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1997; Properly control and account the gasoline consumption and use of motor vehicles; Adopt the standard format of the driver’s trip tickets and ensure that they are properly accomplished (i.e. departure time, arrival time, speedometer reading, name and signature of the passenger; Provide adequate information on the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption of Motor Vehicles and to include significant information like the odometer readings, total distance travelled, distance travelled per liters, and total liters consumed; Prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Requisition and Issue Voucher (RIV) or equivalent using the forms provided; Ensure that no issuance of gasoline from the agency storage tank shall be allowed without an approved RIV or equivalent; For the Municipal Accountant - Properly review the trip tickets submitted by end-users; For the issuing office - Revisit relevant guidelines on how to properly fill-out a driver’s trip ticket and provide only accurate and truthful information on the said form.
Require the Human Resource Development Officer (HRMO) to ensure that the provisions prescribed for a minimum monthly net take pay of P5,000.00 under the GAA FY 2019 and onwards be adhered to.

Direct the OLEEM Manager to ensure that lease applications should be approved after all the requirements are submitted most especially the duly notarized contracts; Recommend to the Mayor for closure of business operation for rabid defiant space renters; Imposed and have collected surcharges on late/delayed rental payments. observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:


     1. Record the adjusting entries to restore  the  amount  of  unreleased
        checks to appropriate cash accounts; and


     2. Review the records of the Municipal Treasurer for unreleased  checks
        at year-end.


        Direct the Municipal Treasurer to:


     3. Prepare a  summary  of  unreleased  checks  for  submission  to  the
        Municipal  Accountant  to  facilitate  the   preparation   of   bank
        reconciliation statements.

We recommended Management to:

    1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare  adjusting  Journal  Entry
       Voucher (JEV) to correct/reclassify the affected accounts; and


    2. Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the prepaid portion  of
       the insurance premiums paid and  to  record  the  Insurance  Expenses
       Account upon consummation  of  the  insurance  services  every  month
       thereafter.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:

   1. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Office or the Municipal
      Engineering Office for the immediate preparation  of  the  documentary
      requirements and  the submission thereof to the  concerned  office  as
      required  in the 6.5.4 of the DILG Memorandum Circular; and


   2. Ensure strict compliance with the  relevant  provisions  of  the  DILG
      Memorandum Circular No. 2019-71 dated May 20,  2019  relative  to  the
      implementation of  Local  Government  Support  Fund  -  Assistance  to
      Municipalities Program.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

   1. Consider redirecting priorities from regular programs  and  activities
      to other  major  concerns  that  need  immediate  action  by  the  LGU
      especially purchasing of lots for relocation sites of  those  homeless
      victims of calamity;


   2.  Require  the  LDRRM  Officer  to  fully  implement  on  CY  2020  the
      unimplemented  projects,  programs  and  activities  of   the   Annual
      Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan and  Budget  for  CY
      2019; and


   3.    Maximize    the    utilization    of    LDRRMF    by    formulating
      programs/projects/activities particularly for  disaster  preparedness,
      prevention and mitigation so that  the  LGU’s  ultimate  objective  of
      protecting the lives  and  properties  of  its  constituents  will  be
      realized.

We recommended Management to:


    1. Maintain disposal site records containing the  monitoring  of  water
       and air quality;


    2. Conduct or sponsor formal trainings on proper solid waste  management
       for  the  site  operators,  garbage  collectors  and  other  relevant
       personnel;


    3. Submit a written notification containing  the  names,  addresses  and
       contact  numbers  of  sanitary  landfill  site   personnel   to   the
       appropriate agencies;


    4.   Reconstitute the SWM Board including the following members:


         • Representative from the recycling industry; and
         • Representative from the manufacturing or packaging industry


       However, if some cannot be included after exhausting all efforts, the
       Management can submit their justifications in writing;


    5. Designate only one area for waste sorting and maintain cleanliness at
       all times within the perimeter of the RCA facility; and


    6.  Procure  appropriate  protective  equipment   for   those   directly
       performing solid waste management operations.

We reiterated our recommendations to the Municipal Mayor to:

      1. Direct the LEEMO to ensure that lease  applications  should  only  be
         approved  after  all  the  requirements   (e.g.   duly   notarized
         contracts) are submitted and to cause the  renewal  of  the  lease
         contracts of all existing occupants, have them duly notarized, and
         ensure that requirements for such renewal are complied with; and


      2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer  and  the  LEEMO  to  effect  sound
         management and internal control policies in the market.

We recommended that the Local Chief executive require  the  GAD  Focal
Person to submit a copy of the DILG approved  annual  GAD  Plan  and  Budget
(GPB) and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report to the COA Audit  Team
within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

   1. Require the Human Resource Development Officer (HRMO) to  ensure  that
      the provisions prescribed for  a  minimum  monthly  net  take  pay  of
      P5,000.00 under the GAA FY 2019 and onwards be adhered to.

   2. Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly  enforce  the  provisions
      stipulated in the GAA pertaining to net take-home pay  to  promote  an
      efficient and effective public service  by  ensuring  that  government
      employees have enough resources  to  support  their  daily  sustenance
      until the next pay day.


   3. Advise the concerned employees to pay and/or  transact  directly  with
      the private institutions, if they opted to  avail  private  loans,  so
      that their net take-home pay will comply with  the  provision  of  the
      GAA.

We recommended Management to:

  1. Require the offices/officials concerned to identify  the  gaps  in  the
     determination and implementation of projects and thereby come  up  with
     corrective measures to  ensure optimum utilization of funds and  timely
     delivery of  value-added services and benefits to constituents; and


  2. Revisit the procurement process of the Municipality that is  identified
     as one of the causes for delayed implementation.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

   1. In coordination with the Offices  of  the  Municipal  Accountant,  the
      General Services and the Rural Health  Unit,  establish  a  procedural
      mechanism from the receipt of the delivery of the  transferred  assets
      from the Department of Health and the Provincial Health Unit up to the
      recording of these assets in the LGU’s books;


   2.  Require  the  Municipal  Health  Officer  to  furnish  the  Municipal
      Accounting Office with the Inventory Report of  the  Drugs,  Medicines
      and Medical Supplies  as  at  December  31,  2018  to  facilitate  the
      recording of the inventories on hand as of year-end; and


   3. For items delivered by PHO, instruct the Accountant to coordinate with
      the PHO, PGSO and/or PAO to indicate  in  their  deliveries  the  unit
      price of inventory items to facilitate recording the books.

Recommendation:

      We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to henceforth perform  loan
aging and analysis and maintain a separate record thereof, aside  from  the
output of the accounting system, to accurately identify  and  classify  the
Loans Payable account in accordance with the specific provisions  in  PPSAS
and PD 1445.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Development
Council to rectify the errors of recording of the particular  project  into
the 20% development fund in the AIP.

      We also recommended that projects/programs that will  be  included  in
the local  budget  shall  be  presented  in  details  such  as  nature  and
particulars of the programs/projects and the recipients, among  others,  in
the AIP, be it the original  or  the  supplemental  AIP  to  provide  full,
complete and precise information regarding those projects/programs.

      Lastly, we recommended that minutes of all the MDC meetings  shall  be
provided with written evidences of the important  details  that  transpired
during the meetings.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

        1. Direct the OLEEM  Manager  to  ensure  that  lease  applications
           should be approved after all the requirements are submitted most
           especially the duly notarized contracts; and


       2.   Direct the OLEEM Manager to effect sound management and internal
           control policies  in  the  market  and  slaughterhouse  such  as
           assigning personnel who will monitor the renewal or  signing  of
           the lease agreement/contract,  check  the  completeness  of  the
           documentary requirements and to review the terms and  conditions
           specifically the lease term before signing the contract.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

       a) Direct the Local Economic Enterprise  to  strictly  implement  the
          payment of surcharge for the late payments and to  disseminate  to
          all lessees of the market on the imposition of surcharges for late
          payments in accordance with the Municipal Ordinance; and


       b) Address the personnel complement deficiency in the local  economic
          enterprise  to  have  maximum  efficiency  and  effectiveness   in
          generating revenues from market operation.

Recommendation:

      We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the MPDO, as well as  the
other departments involved in the implementation of  development  fund,  to
establish a system or process that facilitates monitoring of implementation
of  barangay  projects  funded  from  the  20%  development  fund  of   the
municipality,  to   ensure   optimal   utilization   of   funds   vis-à-vis
accomplishments. The system should clearly define the role and function  of
each office to pinpoint responsibility.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Municipal Mayor to erase the ambiguity and identify
the office who will be overall in-charge in the operation of the PWS.

      We also recommended that the project status of the PWS be  determined,
improvement or upgrading to be undertaken if necessary in  order  that  the
immediate utilization be made and its intended  purpose  be  delivered  and
benefits be felt by the beneficiaries.

detailed information beyond the four-year  period  and  for  the  Municipal
Accountant to properly reflect collections on the corresponding  year  that
the  RPT  and  SET  receivables  were  initially  set-up,  thereby,   avoid
receivable with abnormal balance.

Lastly, we recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to revisit the provisions
in the RA No. 7160 regarding the collection of RPT and SET  Receivables  to
enable her to acquire more confidence from the Act, to implement  a  proper
and high efficiency collection rate on the receivables.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Treasurer  to
properly coordinate with the Assessor’s and Accountant’s  offices  so  that
the preparation of pertinent reports  and  documents  are  coursed  through
proper offices, in order to come up with a prompt basis for the  Tax  dues,
RPT and SET Receivables during  the  year.  Likewise,  we  recommended  the
strict adherence to the above-mentioned guidelines.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to henceforth strictly adhere to
the perpetual inventory method of recording.

Moreover, we  recommended  to  the  Municipal  Treasurer  as  well  as  the
inventory committee including the Municipal Service Officer  to  conduct  a
thorough  and  complete  physical  count  of  the  all  inventory  accounts
especially the subject inventory accounts which were found non-moving.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal  Engineer  to
reevaluate the statuses of completion of the above projects and prepare the
necessary Certificate of Final Completion on each  project/program/activity
to support the reclassification to the proper asset account.

Also, we recommended to the Municipal Accountant  to  prepare  the  journal
entry for the transfer once the certificate from the Municipal  Engineering
Office is issued.
Finally, we recommended that proper coordination be made between offices  so
that deficiencies such as the subject-matter  will  be  primarily  addressed
with.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to
confirm to the Department of Agriculture if  the  assistance  was  actually
converted into a grant as claimed by the former Municipal Agriculturist and
if it is not, immediate liquidation of the obligation of  the  Municipality
be promptly taken.

Also, we also recommended to the responsible officers to strictly adhere to
the accounting and reporting guidelines of the Fund Transfers  to  POs/NGOs
and ensure that all advances are properly liquidated on time.

We recommended to the Municipal Development Council through  the  Municipal
Mayor to ensure effective and proper planning on the utilization of the 20%
Development Fund for the succeeding years.

We also recommended to the Municipal Mayor to fully maximize utilization of
the 20% Development  Fund  for  the  implementation  of  various  projects,
programs and activities that are envisioned to help in  the  attainment  of
desirable socio-economic development  and  environmental  outcomes  and  in
accordance to the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor as the MDRRMC  Chairperson  thru  the
MDRRMO - Designate to facilitate the inclusion of community-based trainings
and information drive in the succeeding MDRRMF Investment Plan  to  enhance
to  the  community’s  knowledge  and  capabilities  on  the   preparedness,
prevention and mitigation man-made and/or natural disasters.

Also, we recommended to Municipal Mayor to require the  Municipal  Disaster
Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to reevaluate and  reassess  the
Investment Plan of  CY  2017  especially  on  the  programs,  projects  and
activities for disaster mitigation and determine the  constraints  for  the
non-implementation so this should  serve  as  one  of  the  bases  for  the
effective and efficient  planning  of  disaster  risk  reduction  projects,
programs and activities in CY 2018 and onwards.

Finally, we recommended for  the  strict  adherence  of  the  above  stated
guidelines.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the  designate  Municipal
Environment and Natural Resources Officer to enforce, monitor and  evaluate
the compliance on the wearing of  the  complete,  basic  safety  gears  and
equipment to all personnel that are directly dealing with  the  collections
of solid wastes of the Municipality.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant  to
ensure the completeness of the  supporting  documents  of  all  transactions
undertaken by the Municipality. In particular, every disbursement should  be
evidenced with an  appropriate  receipt  and  should  contain  the  required
minimum data. Also, strict adherence to the above-mentioned circular  should
be properly observed.

We recommended to the Municipality to allocate  at  least  1%  of  its  IRA
solely for the strengthening and implementation of programs,  projects  and
activities of the LCPC in compliance with the provisions mentioned  in  the
RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July  4,  2012,
in order that prevention, rehabilitation and reintegration of  children  at
risk and/or  in  conflict  with  the  law  are  ensured  and  given  utmost
importance.

Recommendations: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to ascertain that the
utilization of the 20% DF is appropriately maximized and to also ensure that effective and
regular monitoring is instituted on the implementation/completion of barangay
development projects to achieve desirable development and environmental management
outcomes.

We also recommended to the Municipal Engineer to include the projects supported by the
Municipality in the PPA Status Report to aide in the monitoring of project implementations
and also in the conduct of the audit.

Recommendations: The foregoing being considered, we recommended the following:
1. Submit list of specific PPAs funded out of the 20% Development Fund appearing
in the CY 2016’s AIP as loan amortization and Barangay Development Projects.
2. Henceforth, the PPAs to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund for the
coming years’ AIP should be specific in order to enhance the transparency and
accountability in the Municipality’s implementation of development projects
funded therein.

Recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to study and reevaluate the
MDRRMP 2016 and determine the constraints/reasons for the minimal implementation of
the plans and programs specified, particularly disaster prevention, mitigation and disaster
preparedness programs. This should serve as one of the bases for an effective and efficient
planning of disaster risk reduction programs, projects and activities in 2017 and onwards.

Recommendations: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Solid Waste personnel, especially the MSWDO, to immediately implement the proper
segregation from barangay level up to the disposal of solid wastes in the landfill in
accordance with RA No. 9003.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the Municipal Solid Waste
Management Board, ensure that there is proper and sufficient budget allocation relative to
the MSWM’s 10-year plan so that projects and programs are implemented in the year that
they are supposed to be implemented.

Recommendations: We recommended that the DepEd District Supervision strictly adhere
to the priority structure in the utilization of the SEF which shall be the basis of the Local
School Board’s consideration as defined under RA No. 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG
Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998, and subsequent amendments (Joint Circular Nos. 01-A, s.
2000 and 01-B, s. 2001) in order to maximize the benefits that the public schools could
avail.

We also recommended to the responsible officers of the Local School Board to submit an
explanation on why the said expenses were charged to the SEF.

the procurement process; and check the implementation of awarded and implemented
contracts of the Municipality, we recommended the following:

office.

Treasurer to observe timeliness in the remittance of the provincial share of the SEF
collection which is within 15 days of every succeeding month. The Municipal Treasurer

responsibilities on the handling of SEF collections.

within the prescribed period so as to avoid receipt of Notice of Suspensions/
Disallowance; and to evaluate the existing accounting system and personnel
complement if still responsive to the growing demands of the present operations of

observations.recommendation:
In order to foster public awareness and monitoring of the procurement process and implementation of perfected and awarded infrastructure contracts of the Municipal Government, we recommend the following:

1. To require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) for all types of procurements at the end of each semester and have it approved by the HOPE; and

We recommend that the BAC:

1. Submit proof of duly served invitations to mandated observers in all stages aside from the pre-bid conference and opening of bids. In the event none were made, written justification may be submitted.

2. Henceforth, strictly comply with RA 9184 and its IRR and make part of contract documents proof of duly served written invitations to mandated observers in all stages of procurements that will be undertaken by the Municipality.

We recommend that:

1. The agency must strictly adhere to the RA 10121 in appropriating the fund and in the implementations of projects and programs under the Calamity Fund. 

2. The Municipal Accountant must comply with the accounting changes stated in the cited circular and strictly adhere to proper recording of the equipment acquired under the LDRRM Fund.  Adjusting entry should be made to recognize/reclassify assets acquired under LDRRM program.
